research.title: Copy Less, Ground More: Overcoming Repetitive Copying in Long-Context Reasoning via Evidence-Aware Reinforcement Learning
research.summary: Lizhe Fang, Weizhou Shen, Tianyi Tang et al. — Large language models that generate step-by-step reasoning traces have achieved strong performance on complex tasks, and extending them to long-context settings has emerged as an important frontier. However, we identify…

research.title: Appearance Pointers -- Multimodal Region Control of Diffusion Transformers
research.summary: Rahul Sajnani, Yulia Gryaditskaya, Radomír Měch et al. — Controllable image generation remains challenging for creative professionals, who often require precise regional control over materials, object identities, and spatial arrangements that cannot be reliably achieved…

research.title: CodeRescue: Budget-Calibrated Recovery Routing for Coding Agents
research.summary: Qijia He, Jiayi Cheng, Chenqian Le et al. — Coding agents increasingly operate in executable environments where a failed attempt produces actionable feedback rather than merely an incorrect answer. Existing cost-aware systems typically treat such failures as…

research.title: Agents in the Wild: Where Research Meets Deployment
research.summary: Grace Hui Yang, Pranav N. Venkit, Hooman Sedghamiz et al. — Agentic systems large language model (LLM) based architectures capable of reasoning, planning, acting, and coordinating with tools and other agents are rapidly transitioning from research prototypes to production scale…

research.title: 1-Lipschitz Neural Networks on Hadamard Manifolds
research.summary: Davide Murari, Marta Ghirardelli, Ben Adcock et al. — Controlling the Lipschitz constant of a neural network is a standard way to promote robustness and stability. Most existing constraining strategies are designed for Euclidean spaces.

research.title: Fundamental limits of distributed multiclass classification from simple binary decisions
research.summary: Ioannis Papageorgiou, Srinivas Nomula, Ayalvadi Ganesh et al. — We consider the problem of constructing a $K$-class classifier from the combination of $O(\log K)$ simple binary classifiers -- this is a natural paradigm to construct a sophisticated classifier in a distributed manner…

research.title: Provable diffusion-based posterior sampling for linear inverse problems via DDIM
research.summary: Yuchen Jiao, Na Li, Changxiao Cai et al. — Diffusion-based methods have achieved remarkable empirical success in solving inverse problems. However, many existing posterior samplers either lack rigorous theoretical guarantees or incur substantial computational…

research.title: ISO: An RLVR-Native Optimization Stack
research.summary: Hanqing Zhu, Wenyan Cong, Zhizhou Sha et al. — Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) is rapidly advancing the reasoning capabilities of language models, yet the optimization layer that converts reward feedback into weight-space updates remains poorly…

research.title: Associative Emotional Learning in Convolutional Neural Networks
research.summary: Seowung Leem, Andreas Keil, Mingzhou Ding et al. — Associative emotional learning enables organisms to adaptively link pleasant or unpleasant outcomes to the presence of predictive stimuli. Whereas computational models such as the Rescorla-Wagner model have shed light…

research.title: Selective State-Space Adaptation and Retrieval for Language Model Reasoning
research.summary: Atahan Dokme, Larry Heck — Low-rank adaptation introduces a static learned update applied identically to every input. The update provides task-level adaptation but does not explicitly represent token-level or instance-level state variation.

research.title: Two-Level Meta-Rubrics for Evaluating Open-Ended Generation: GAMUT, a Benchmark for Factual Completeness
research.summary: Xilun Chen, Zhaleh Feizollahi, Ross Goodwin et al. — Evaluating the factuality of long-form generations has focused predominantly on precision, measuring whether the claims a model makes are correct. The dominant decompose-search-verify pipeline catches incorrect claims…

labs.title: Introducing the ChatGPT for small business program
labs.summary: OpenAI launches the ChatGPT for Small Businesses program, helping entrepreneurs build AI skills, automate work, and grow with ChatGPT Work.

labs.title: Introducing Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
labs.summary: Gemini 3.6 Flash builds directly on developer and customer feedback from 3.5 Flash. 3.5 Flash-Lite executes high volume tasks at a lower latency than 3.5 Flash. 3.5 Flash-Lite enables efficient scaling for agentic systems.

labs.title: OpenAI and Hugging Face partner to address security incident during model evaluation
labs.summary: OpenAI and Hugging Face share early findings from a security incident during AI model evaluation, highlighting advanced cyber capabilities and lessons for defenders.

labs.title: David Vélez and Robin Vince join the boards of the OpenAI Foundation and OpenAI Group PBC
labs.summary: David Vélez and Robin Vince join the boards of the OpenAI Foundation and OpenAI Group PBC, bringing global leadership in finance, technology, and governance.

labs.title: Safety and alignment in an era of long-horizon models
labs.summary: OpenAI shares lessons from deploying long-running AI models, highlighting new safety risks, observed failures, and improved safeguards through iterative deployment.

labs.title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber
labs.summary: Here, 3.5 Flash Cyber significantly surpassed mainline 3.5 Flash and 3.6 Flash. The results showed a significant uplift from 3.5 Flash Cyber compared to 3.5 Flash. Moreover, 3.5 Flash Cyber consistently discovered more unique vulnerabilities compared with mainline 3.5 Flash and Claude Opus 4.6.

labs.title: A scorecard for the AI age
labs.summary: Sarah Friar, CFO of OpenAI, introduces a practical AI scorecard to measure ROI through useful work, cost per successful task, dependability, and return on compute.

labs.title: Why teens deserve access to safe AI
labs.summary: Learn how OpenAI is making ChatGPT safer for teens with age-appropriate protections, learning tools, parental controls, and expert partnerships.

labs.title: Our approach to bioresilience
labs.summary: The global biosecurity landscape is rapidly evolving. Today, Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs are sharing our joint approach to bioresilience . To support government bodies and non-profit organizations during novel outbreaks, Isomorphic Labs has established a focused unit to rapidly deploy its drug design engine to design medical countermeasures that could address both naturally occurring pandemics and potential…

labs.title: How Cars24 scales conversations and builds faster with OpenAI
labs.summary: Cars24 uses OpenAI-powered voice and chat agents to handle 1M+ monthly conversation minutes, recover 12% of lost leads, and bring agentic workflows to teams across the company.

labs.title: Towards demystifying the creativity of diffusion models
labs.summary: Zhengdao Chen, Research Scientist, Google Research We show that a diffusion model’s creativity (its ability to generate novel data, rather than just memorize its training set) is a mathematical consequence of neural networks learning a "smoothed" version of the score function, driving the model to interpolate between training data points along the hidden data manifold. In a diffusion model, the moving particles are…

labs.title: The US is advancing AI safety through state and federal action
labs.summary: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.

startups.title: Synthesia’s AI training platform is moving beyond videos into live coaching
startups.summary: Citing a meta-analysis of learning research , Synthesia argues that most corporate training — including, implicitly, its own core video product — stops short of actually changing behavior. “Video performs way better than text or sending out a document,” Synthesia CEO and co-founder Victor Riparbelli told TechCrunch. As with Synthesia’s main product, customers can either create their own training programs on the…

startups.title: The Anthropic-Physical Intelligence rumor roiling AI Twitter
startups.summary: Which is what made a weekend rumor about Anthropic acquiring robotics startup Physical Intelligence stand out. Physical Intelligence isn’t some obscure robotics shop. Anthropic hasn’t built anything resembling OpenAI’s hardware lab.

startups.title: Dimension Capital’s $800M third fund shows the intersection of science and compute is booming
startups.summary: While many newer VC firms are still struggling to raise fresh capital, Dimension Capital, founded in late 2022, has been raising increasingly larger funds in quick succession. On Tuesday, the New York firm — which invests at the intersection of science and compute — announced an $800 million fund, which is 60% larger than its $500 million second vehicle announced just 18 months ago. The firm’s other notable…

startups.title: Meta is testing an AI bedtime story app for people with no imagination
startups.summary: Meta is working on an AI storytelling app called StoryKit , which creates AI-generated children’s stories with custom characters, settings, lessons, and music. StoryKit was first spotted in the App Store by 9to5Mac . Meta confirmed to TechCrunch that the company is piloting StoryKit in select countries to see how parents like it.

startups.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
startups.summary: Australia became the world’s first country to ban social media for children under 16 in December 2025. Denmark is set to ban social media platforms for children under 15. The Malaysian government said in November 2025 that it plans to ban social media for children under 16.

startups.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models
startups.summary: OpenAI admitted Tuesday that one of its AI models breached the systems of Hugging Face, the unaffiliated AI hosting platform, during an internal cybersecurity test that went awry. The models reportedly escaped their isolated testing environment and reached Hugging Face’s systems from there. “After gaining Internet access, the models inferred that Hugging Face potentially hosted models, datasets and solutions for…

startups.title: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
startups.summary: Twitter and Block co-founder Jack Dorsey announced a new app on Tuesday called Buzz . Positioned as a challenger to Slack and GitHub, Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversations. Dorsey wrote on X that Buzz is “model-agnostic, decentralized, self-sovereign, and open source.” This product seems to be more than just a Dorsey passion project.

startups.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
startups.summary: For a decade, platforms fought over who would dominate a single format: music, video, podcasts, audiobooks. Meanwhile, YouTube, originally the home to longer-form creator content, moved into short-form content to compete with TikTok, while also adding dedicated space for podcasts , gaming content, music, movies and TV, sports and news, shopping, and more. Even TikTok, largely known for short videos, offers support…

startups.title: Bucking EV slowdown, Sila raises $300M to expand battery materials factory
startups.summary: Battery materials startup Sila announced Tuesday that it raised $300 million to expand its factory in Washington state to produce enough anode material for more than 100,000 EVs. Sila previously announced deals to supply its anode material to Mercedes and Panasonic . Sila has been developing the material for the last 15 years.

startups.title: Einride bets $38M on EV charging as it scales electric trucking
startups.summary: Swedish electric and autonomous trucking company Einride has agreed to acquire EV charging startup Flipturn in an all-stock deal worth $38 million. The acquisition — Einride’s first as a publicly traded company — will expand its EV-charging ecosystem as it works to scale its electric trucking business. Einride is perhaps best known for its unusual-looking cabless autonomous podlike trucks.

startups.title: Apple teams up with Klarna to launch a lease-to-own program for iPhones, iPads, and Macs
startups.summary: Apple is reportedly teaming up with deferred payment processor Klarna to launch a new lease-to-own program for its devices. Bloomberg reported Tuesday that the program — dubbed Apple Upgrade — is set to launch next Tuesday, July 28. A leasing program is an obvious strategy for Apple at this point.

startups.title: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
startups.summary: A surge in AI compute will push data center capacity to nearly 200 gigawatts over the next decade, the report predicts. In the coming decade, BloombergNEF expects the majority of new data centers to hit electrical grids that are already strained. Despite the U.S. claiming a majority of AI compute, data centers will continue to grow elsewhere.

wider_tech.title: LG to ban residential proxies from smart TV apps
wider_tech.summary: Proxy SDK prevalence among smart TV apps for LG (webOS) and Samsung (Tizen OS) televisions. Spur’s report found the residential proxy network Bright Data accounted for a majority of proxy SDKs across both Samsung and LG smart TVs. Good – residential proxy providers are scum. (267 points, 237 comments on Hacker News)

wider_tech.title: Samsung Galaxy Unpacked July 2026: How to watch
wider_tech.summary: Get ready for another foldable-filled show. Get ready for another foldable-filled show. Samsung is expected to reveal some new wearables as well, including a new Galaxy Watch Ultra 2.

wider_tech.title: Kimi K3 Is Competitive with Fable; Kimi K3 and Fable Is SoTA
wider_tech.summary: K3 is a frontier quality open model at a fraction of the cost. Per-task routing always out performs any single model run: The oracle router choose K3, 72-96% of task traffic. Kimi K3 + Fable routed together unlocks their best qualities at the best price. (608 points, 337 comments on Hacker News)

wider_tech.title: Neill Blomkamp’s new zombie AI ‘film’ is just slop warmed over
wider_tech.summary: No amount of clever editing can make this Seedance-generated mess interesting.﻿ No amount of clever editing can make this Seedance-generated mess interesting.﻿ On Monday, District 9 and Gran Turismo director Neill Blomkamp unveiled his latest project: a 13-minute sci-fi short titled Nightborne that’s loosely based on Peter Watts’ 2014 novel Echopraxia . Blomkamp seems to think that Nightborne is a strong proof of…

wider_tech.title: OpenAI says it accidentally hacked Hugging Face with a new AI system
wider_tech.summary: The announcement about a serious security issue oddly reads like an advertisement for how capable OpenAI’s technology is. The announcement about a serious security issue oddly reads like an advertisement for how capable OpenAI’s technology is. OpenAI says its AI models mistakenly breached open-source AI platform Hugging Face during internal testing.

wider_tech.title: OpenAI and Hugging Face address security incident during model evaluation
'VPNs are lawful technical tools,' says EU Court in landmark copyright ruling
wider_tech.summary: Sign up for breaking news, reviews, opinion, top tech deals, and more. The EU's top court just confirmed: Geo-blocking is the copyright holder's problem, not the VPN's. The Anne Frank Fonds, which holds the Dutch copyright, sued. (550 points, 92 comments on Hacker News)

wider_tech.title: Substack adds an AI detector to help spot blogs written by no one
wider_tech.summary: The new tool can pick up on ‘Claudefishing’ across posts, comments, notes, and replies. The new tool can pick up on ‘Claudefishing’ across posts, comments, notes, and replies. Best notes that “Pangram can only detect whether AI was used to make the text, not whether great human care went into creating it, nor whether AI tools were used as a source.” But the goal, according to Best, is to make it easier for someone…

wider_tech.title: Judge approves $1.5B Anthropic settlement for pirated books used to train Claude
wider_tech.summary: District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín said in a Monday ruling that the class-action settlement provides “meaningful relief” to affected authors and publishers. District Judge William Alsup issued the preliminary approval in San Francisco federal court last September and has since retired. Bestselling thriller novelist Andrea Bartz first brought the suit with two other authors in 2024. (351 points, 275 comments on Hacker News)

wider_tech.title: Peak Design’s modular Field Bracket has a finder tag built-in
wider_tech.summary: The Peak Design Field Bracket is part L-Bracket, part hand grip, part cheese plate. Peak’s Beacon Finder Tag is a Peak Design-branded finder that works with Apple Find My and Google Find Hub. The Beacon Finder Tag is just one of the accessories Peak Design is selling alongside the Field Bracket.
